FactionColonies.FCEventDef.label: Disputed Spring
FactionColonies.FCEventDef.desc: A natural spring has been discovered at the boundary between your settlement and a neighboring community. Both sides have been drawing water from it, but supplies are dwindling. The neighbors are fortifying their claim. Left unresolved, this could get ugly fast.

FactionColonies.FCOptionDef.label: Send an envoy to negotiate. There's enough water for everyone if we're reasonable about it.
Post guards at the spring. That water is ours and they know it.
Hire prospectors to find our own water source. We don't need their spring.

FactionColonies.FCEventDef.label: At the Table
FactionColonies.FCEventDef.desc: Your envoy sits down with the neighboring community's leaders. Talks are tense but productive. Both sides agree to a temporary moratorium on drawing water while terms are hammered out. Time will tell whether diplomacy holds.

FactionColonies.FCEventDef.label: Escalation
FactionColonies.FCEventDef.desc: The situation at the spring has deteriorated. Armed settlers from both sides are staring each other down across a drying creek bed. Scuffles have broken out. One wrong move and this becomes a full-blown conflict. What are your orders?

FactionColonies.FCOptionDef.label: Pull our people back. This isn't worth dying over.
Send in the militia. If they want a fight, they'll get one.
Offer a generous payment for full water rights. Money talks louder than weapons.

FactionColonies.FCEventDef.label: Retreat from the Spring
FactionColonies.FCEventDef.desc: Your settlers withdraw from the disputed spring under the jeering of the neighboring community. The settlement loses access to the water source entirely. Morale plummets as word spreads that your nation backed down from a fight.

FactionColonies.FCEventDef.label: Water Seized by Force
FactionColonies.FCEventDef.desc: Your militia overwhelms the neighboring settlers in a brief but brutal confrontation. The spring is yours — but the violence leaves a mark. The neighbors won't forget this, and your own people are unsettled by the bloodshed. Still, the water flows freely now.

FactionColonies.FCEventDef.label: New Wells
FactionColonies.FCEventDef.desc: Your prospectors strike water deep underground — it's not as convenient as the disputed spring, but it's yours alone. During the weeks of drilling and construction, food production dips as the old source becomes unreliable. Once the wells are operational, the settlement will be self-sufficient.

FactionColonies.FCEventDef.label: Uneasy Peace
FactionColonies.FCEventDef.desc: A fragile agreement is in place: both communities share the spring under strict schedules. Nobody is happy about it, and the reduced access means less water for irrigation. The question is whether the neighbors will honor the deal — or find their own solution.

FactionColonies.FCEventDef.label: Neighbors Find Their Own Source
FactionColonies.FCEventDef.desc: Word arrives that the neighboring community has tapped into their own underground water source. They no longer need the disputed spring. The sharing agreement dissolves naturally, and your settlement regains full access. The crisis is over.

FactionColonies.FCEventDef.label: Permanent Water Sharing
FactionColonies.FCEventDef.desc: The temporary arrangement has hardened into a permanent one. Neither side is willing to renegotiate, and the shared schedule is now simply how things are done. Food production takes a lasting hit.
